_comment: Phase 10 — all on-screen text for the 3D scenes. Plain language only. No jargon. Every label must pass the 'random visitor understands it in 3 seconds' test. Change these freely; code reads at runtime.

philosophies.conventional: IF WE KEEP FARMING THE WAY WE DO NOW
philosophies.rewild: IF WE LET NATURE TAKE IT BACK

facts: One teaspoon of healthy soil holds more living things than there are people on Earth.
Soil stores three times more carbon than all the air in the atmosphere combined.
It takes nature 1,000 years to make 3 cm of topsoil — we can lose it in five.
A single earthworm moves up to 9 kg of soil per year, ploughing it for free.
Healthy soil holds water like a sponge: up to 20 times its own weight.
Half of all the carbon stored on land lives underground, in roots and soil.
One gram of soil can contain over a billion bacteria — a city under your fingernail.
Beneath one footstep of forest, fungal threads stretch for kilometers like an underground internet.
More species live in a handful of garden soil than in all the trees above it.
If soil microbes vanished, every plant on Earth would die within a year.

attract_loop.intro: Three patches of ground. Same place. Same fifty years. Three very different futures.

scene_c_healthy.title: The ground is alive.
scene_c_healthy.fact: One teaspoon of this soil holds more living things than there are people on Earth.

scene_c_dying.title: The ground is empty.
scene_c_dying.fact: When soil dies it stops being soil — it becomes dust the wind carries away.
